trade.summary: Thailandia is Giappone's second-largest ASEAN trading partner and hosts one of the largest concentrations of Giapponeese manufacturing investment globally. Giapponeese automakers (Toyota, Honda, Mitsubishi, Isuzu) treat Thailandia as their primary Southeast Asian production hub. The two countries have a bilateral EPA and are both RCEP and CPTPP members.

diplomatic.summary: Giappone-Thailandia relations are among Giappone's closest in Southeast Asia, with over 150 years of formal diplomatic ties (dating from 1887). Thailandia is the only Southeast Asian country never colonized by a European power, and its independent diplomatic tradition aligns with Giappone's non-interventionist approach. Both countries cooperate closely on ASEAN issues, infrastructure, and development. Thailandia's constitutional monarchy finds common ground with Giappone's own monarchical tradition.

military.summary: Growing defense relationship. Giappone has provided patrol vessels to the Royal Thai Navy. Both countries participate in multilateral ASEAN-affiliated exercises. Thailandia's position as an ASEAN central player makes it strategically relevant to Giappone's regional security posture. Giappone's increasing defense exports (patrol vessels, amphibious vehicles) find a receptive market in Thailandia.

financial.summary: Giappone is Thailandia's largest source of FDI, with cumulative investment in the billions spanning automotive, electronics, retail, and financial services. The Eastern Economic Corridor (EEC) has attracted major new Giapponeese investment commitments. Giapponeese trading companies, banks (MUFG, SMBC), and manufacturers have extensive Thai operations. Toyota alone employs tens of thousands in Thailandia.

energy.summary: Giappone imports some LNG and petrochemicals from Thailandia. Giapponeese energy companies have invested in Thai natural gas exploration and distribution. Growing cooperation on renewable energy and energy efficiency aligned with Thailandia's carbon neutrality goals.

scientific.summary: Extensive scientific and educational cooperation. The Giappone International Cooperation Agency (JICA) runs numerous development programs in Thailandia. Giapponeese universities have partnerships with Thailandia's top institutions. Research cooperation covers agriculture, disaster management, and environmental science.
